---
title: jupyter
---

```{css css_custom, code=readLines("_css_custom.css"), echo=FALSE}
```
```{r knitr_config, code=readLines("_knitr_config.R"), echo=FALSE}
```

*********************************************************************

> an open-source web application that allows you to create and share documents that contain live code, equations, visualizations and narrative text

> Jupyter stands for Julia, Python, and R

*********************************************************************

* https://jupyter.org/  
* https://www.datacamp.com/community/tutorials/tutorial-jupyter-notebook

*********************************************************************

## installation 

jupyter notebook is included in the anaconda distribution - see notes on [conda](https://dkidney.github.io/how2/conda.html)

however, here are some options if you need to install it manaully:

```sh
python3 -m pip install jupyter
python -m pip install jupyter
conda install jupyter
```

*********************************************************************

## kernels

The ipython kernel is the python execution backend for jupyter

```sh
# list all kernels
jupyter kernelspec list

# add a new kernel using conda
conda activate py2
conda install ipykernel
python -m ipykernel install --user --name py2 --display-name "py2"

# add a new kernel using pip
python2 -m pip --version # need pip version >= 9
python2 -m pip install ipykernel
python2 -m ipykernel install --user

# delete a kernel
jupyter kernelspec uninstall unwanted-kernel
```

*********************************************************************

## magic commands

ipython allows you to execute shell commands directly via  magic commands - e.g. 

```sh
! ls
%cd
! pwd
```
